pages.raw_text:
Members of local railway unions
state that the action of the Gould
lines in regulating contracts with
labor organizations is due directly
to the recent decision of the U. S.
supreme court.


The court has ruled that men,
bers of unions employed on rail
ways could be blacklisted and boy
cotted, but that the unions could
not boycott the railroad. Under
this ruling, it is claimed, that
should a strike be called in this
state and the members of the union
strike in Idaho and Montana the
men in those state could be prose
cuted for aiding the men in this
state and engaging in a boycott.
The railway. under the recent rul-
ing of the court, could boycott and
blacklist every man engaged in the
strike, or who aided the strikers in
any manner.


1t is claimed that the decisions
rendered are directly responsible
for the sudden activity on the part
of the Rio Grande for the establish
ment of the open shop, and it will
not surprise the unions to see SINH
ar action taken by other roads at
an early date.
